place: Bay Shore New York, New York

facts.text: Bay Shore’s historic Sagtikos Manor, built in 1697, served as a British headquarters during the Battle of Long Island and hosted President Washington in 1790.
The Bay Shore Historical Society, founded in 1985 by Mary Rogers McDonald, preserves the town’s heritage and houses its research archives on Maple Avenue.
Bay Shore High School, an IB‑authorized campus offering AP and diploma programs, has produced notable alumni such as Grammy‑winning singer Patti Austin.
The town’s Long Island Rail Road station, a key access point for the Fire Island ferry, features an elevated walkway that eases passenger flow.
In 1994, the Diane Collins Macelroy Memorial Library opened in the historic Gibson‑Mack‑Holt House, thanks to society charter member Priscilla Hancock.
Bay Shore’s Great South Bay shoreline has long attracted summer visitors, including author Mario Puzo, who penned parts of “The Godfather” while staying there.
